title: Delivery OS Execution Node Contract
description: Declares what a host MUST provide to be a valid Delivery OS Execution Node. The platform owns this contract; each node publishes a node-instance record asserting which capabilities it has activated. A node is valid iff verify-node.sh passes against this contract. No field names a project, host, or path — those are instance-local.

identity.description: Engineering identity the node executes as. Builder GitHub identity for attribution; a dedicated, non-admin OS user for process isolation. Never the founder identity, never the human login account.

githubIdentity.description: the builder GitHub login used for git/API attribution

osRunnerUser.description: dedicated non-admin OS user that runs execution services

notFounder.description: MUST be true — execution never runs as the founder identity or the human login user

toolchain.description: Minimum toolchain a node must provide. Versions are floors; a node may exceed them but must honour per-project pins.

node.description: must satisfy the stack pin (e.g. 22.x); managed by a version manager so per-project pins are honoured

capabilities.description: The capabilities the Execution Layer defines. Each is optional to ACTIVATE on a given node, but if activated must satisfy its sub-contract. 'workstation' and 'localCI' need no grant; 'runner','heartbeat','mesh' are operator-gated.

localCI.description: run build + independent VERIFY locally, zero hosted-CI minutes

scope.description: repo-level (smallest blast radius) or an allow-listed org runner group

ephemeral.description: MUST be ephemeral — one job per registration

privateOnly.description: MUST refuse public repos / fork PRs

heartbeat.description: always-on host that drives the execution-engine tick at a real interval (what serverless daily-cron tiers cannot honour)

mesh.description: secure overlay network for remote/inter-node access

default: execution never runs as the founder identity or the human login user
high-value secrets (prod DB, signing keys, money/PII creds) are kept off the self-hosted scope
no platform-canonical state lives only on a node; a node is reconstructable from this layer + operator grants
the runner refuses public repos and untrusted fork PRs
